title: attribution — kill switches
description: Operational kill switches distinct from the tier-based `features.json` gates. These are for emergency-off / staged rollout / per-tenant ops interventions. Every flag is ON in normal production; off flips are ops decisions.

flags.description: Master toggle for AttributionMiddleware. Off = middleware becomes a no-op globally; downstream events attribute against null profiles. Emergency-only (used during DB migrations of the attributions table).

Off = middleware still resolves + binds AttributionContext for the request, but skips the DB write. Downstream events still get their snapshot from the resolved (in-memory-only) profile. Used to shed DB load during incidents while keeping downstream flows functional.
Off = ClickIdExtractor returns an empty jsonb. Emergency-only if a specific ad-network's click ID format changes and starts throwing parse errors.
Off = MergeIdentifiedAttributionsJob no-ops. Used when the merge strategy is misbehaving (runaway merges, first-touch overwrite bugs).
Off = CampaignRollupAggregatorJob skips. Used when the rollup pipeline is unstable to avoid backing up its queue.

Off = 'Attribution Touchpoint Captured' analytics event fires on 100% of touchpoints (bypassing the 10% default sample rate). Enterprise tenants may flip on for full-fidelity funnel analysis. Warning: high analytics volume.
Off = ReconcileAttributionCountsJob + PurgeArchivedAttributionsJob skip. Only flip off for maintenance windows.
Off = MarkAttributionChurnedJob no-ops. Used during migration windows where the touchpoint table lags.
Off = AttributionMiddleware ignores the Sec-GPC HTTP header. Emergency-only if a compliance disagreement mid-rollout requires falling back to explicit consent only.
Off = AttributionSuppressedByConsentNotification batches skip. Compliance team can still query via the audit report. Used to reduce notification volume during a large consent-refresh campaign.
